CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Biggert-Waters Flood Insurance Reform Act of 2012, and also as part of the Moving Ahead for Progress in the 21st Century Act, also known as the MAP–21, and not as part of National Flood Insurance Act of 1968 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: 100-year floodplain
children.label: 1 100-year floodplain
children.text: The term “100-year floodplain” means that area which is subject to inundation from a flood having a 1-percent chance of being equaled or exceeded in any given year.

children.heading: 500-year floodplain
children.label: 2 500-year floodplain
children.text: The term “500-year floodplain” means that area which is subject to inundation from a flood having a 0.2-percent chance of being equaled or exceeded in any given year.

children.heading: Administrator
children.label: 3 Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency.

children.heading: National Flood Insurance Program
children.label: 4 National Flood Insurance Program
children.text: The term “National Flood Insurance Program” means the program established under the National Flood Insurance Act of 1968 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 4011

children.heading: Write Your Own
children.label: 5 Write Your Own
children.text: The term “Write Your Own” means the cooperative undertaking between the insurance industry and the Federal Insurance Administration which allows participating property and casualty insurance companies to write and service standard flood insurance policies.

children.heading: Common terminology
children.label: b Common terminology
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subtitle, any terms used in this subtitle shall have the meaning given to such terms under section 1370 of the National Flood Insurance Act of 1968 ().42 U.S.C. 4121
